body.title: /notify skill: set level, show config, list pending
body.result: /notify skill: status/set/pending subcommands; reads/writes NotificationConfig; deployed to ~/.claude/skills/
body.intention: I intend that CC and Akien can inspect and change notification levels from the command line without editing config files manually.
body.description:
Write a /notify skill that provides a CLI for managing notification settings. Commands: `notify status` — show current effective level for this session (default + any active overrides, current agent state), `notify set LOUD|QUIET|SILENT` — write to notifications.cfg default level, `notify set <sender> LOUD|QUIET|SILENT` — write per-sender override, `notify pending` — list messages queued in notify_pending.txt. The skill reads from the same NotificationConfig introduced in T-notif-config-schema.

**Affected files:** skills/notify (new skill file)
**Design rules:** skills live in repo root skills/ — not ~/.claude/skills/
**Scope boundary:** In scope: status/set/pending subcommands, reads/writes notifications.cfg, reads notify_pending.txt. Out of scope: changes to shim dispatch logic, message content display (just sender + timestamp in pending list).
**Completion criteria:** `notify status` prints current level and overrides. `notify set LOUD` updates notifications.cfg and confirms. `notify pending` lists queued senders with timestamps or 'nothing pending'. Skill is callable from bash as `/notify` in CC session.
